hero.title: Security Sprint
hero.subtitle: Two weeks. One focus. Senior-level capability, zero scope creep.

intro.body: A Security Sprint is a highly scoped, time-boxed engagement on one critical area. Not a full assessment. Not a comprehensive blueprint. A focused deep dive that delivers findings, guidance, and a clear path forward — in two weeks.

sprintFormats.title: Assessment Sprint
sprintFormats.focus: Deep-dive review of a specific domain — CI/CD pipeline, IAM architecture, cloud configuration, compliance gap, or any single security area that needs attention now.
deliverables: Focused assessment of the target area
Prioritized findings ranked by business impact
Remediation guidance with clear next steps
sprintFormats.whatThisIsNot: A comprehensive engagement. Sprints are focused and time-boxed. If we discover broader issues during the sprint, we'll flag them — but addressing them requires a separate engagement.

sprintFormats.title: Build Partnership Sprint
sprintFormats.focus: Outcome-driven guidance on a specific implementation — rolling out SSO, hardening a Kubernetes cluster, implementing secrets management, or any focused build where you need senior oversight.
deliverables: Weekly sync calls throughout the sprint
Async access (Slack/email) for decisions and questions
Pair sessions for complex implementation work
Decision log (running document of decisions, rationale, alternatives)
sprintFormats.whatThisIsNot: We don't write your code. We don't take 24/7 on-call responsibility. We guide your team through the implementation — you own the outcome.

sprintFormats.title: Extended Sprint
sprintFormats.focus: For teams that need both the initial assessment and the follow-through guidance. Combines Assessment and Build Partnership into a single extended engagement.
deliverables: Week 1: Focused assessment of the target area
Weeks 2-3: Guided implementation with senior oversight
All deliverables from both Assessment and Build Partnership formats
sprintFormats.whenToChoose: You know you need both assessment and implementation guidance, and you want a single engagement that covers both without the overhead of coordinating two separate sprints.

rulesOfEngagement.title: Before the Clock Starts
rulesOfEngagement.body: We don't start until you're ready. Before the sprint begins, we verify that your team is prepared, the scope is defined, and access is in place. If you're not ready, we pause — because starting a sprint without preparation wastes everyone's time.
requirements: A designated technical contact with decision-making authority
Access to the systems, code, and stakeholders we need
Clarity on the sprint scope and success criteria
Your team's availability for the full sprint duration

rulesOfEngagement.title: Your Team Builds. We Guide.
rulesOfEngagement.body: We require a dedicated, empowered team on your end to work with us in real-time. We are not here to write your code — we are here to guide your execution. The sprint moves fast, and decisions need to be made quickly. If your team isn't available to implement, the sprint can't deliver.

rulesOfEngagement.title: Four Check-ins. No Surprises.

checkins.title: Scope Confirmation
checkins.description: We confirm the scope is right and access is working. No surprises yet.

checkins.title: Mid-Sprint Review
checkins.description: Findings so far, direction check, course correction if needed.

checkins.title: Sprint Close
checkins.description: Findings delivered, guidance documented, path forward clear.

rulesOfEngagement.body: If your team is failing to meet milestones at any check-in, we'll officially pause the sprint to re-align. We'd rather pause and get it right than rush and get it wrong.

rulesOfEngagement.title: The Clock Doesn't Lie
rulesOfEngagement.body: Sprints are strictly time-boxed. If the clock runs out due to internal delays on your end, the sprint is still marked as a success from our side — provided we delivered our assessment, effort, and guidance. Shared responsibility is required. To continue work beyond the sprint, a new sprint must be scheduled.
rulesOfEngagement.closingNote: This isn't a penalty — it's how we maintain quality and prevent scope creep.

sprintDomains.title: What We Can Sprint On
sprintDomains.subtitle: Sprints are focused on a single domain. Here are some areas where a sprint delivers the most value:
sprintDomains.closingNote: Don't see your domain? Every sprint is scoped individually. If you need something specific, let's talk.

sprintVsFull.title: When a Sprint vs. When a Full Engagement

rows.label: Best for
rows.sprint: You know exactly what needs attention
rows.full: You need a comprehensive security program

sprintVsFull.closingNote: Not sure which you need? Start with a Sprint — if we discover broader issues, we'll tell you. And if a full engagement is the right fit, we'll credit the sprint cost toward it.

crossLinks.title: Ready to take the next step?
